structure.govukComponent: Caption (non-standard: span.heading-tertiary inside the H1, not govuk-caption-xl)
Heading (govuk-heading-xl); the visible primary title is span#page-primary-title "Laboratory tests", preceded by the reference-number caption span

structure.text: Would you like to record laboratory tests? / Choose from: / Yes / No
structure.govukComponent: Radios inside a Fieldset with a govuk-fieldset__legend--m legend
Link (govuk-link) in a govuk-body paragraph, href to the CHED hub

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 wrapping a reference-number/version caption span and the primary title 'Laboratory tests'
Groups the Yes/No radio question with legend 'Would you like to record laboratory tests?'
Yes/No choice for whether to record laboratory tests (labTestsRequired, values true/false)
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 123; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 128

govukComponents.usage: Visually-hidden 'Choose from:' hint for the radios; carries a copy-paste id 'container-seals-hint' from another IPAFFS template
Two submit buttons both named 'save-button' — 'Save and return' (value 'Save and return') and 'Save and continue' (value 'Save and continue')
'Cancel' link back to the CHED hub, wrapped in a govuk-body paragraph

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the CHED reference number and version ('CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057 - V1') as a caption line above the primary page title, nested inside the H1 alongside span#page-primary-title 'Laboratory tests'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: 'heading-tertiary' is an IPAFFS-bespoke class, not a GOV.UK Design System class, and the caption is placed inside the H1 rather than as a sibling — screen readers read the reference number as part of the heading. The only non-govuk-* class on the page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-caption-xl as a sibling span above the H1 (the standard GOV.UK caption+heading pattern), so the caption is not part of the accessible heading name.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Provides the visually-hidden 'Choose from:' hint associated with the radios.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The id 'container-seals-hint' is a leaked copy-paste artifact from an unrelated IPAFFS 'container seals' template — the markup is govuk-standard but the id is misleading/dead. Not a component concern, but a code-hygiene signal the rebuild should not carry over.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk Radios hint with a page-appropriate id (or drop it — 'Choose from:' is redundant with a Yes/No radio group).

fields.name: labTestsRequired
fields.label: Would you like to record laboratory tests?
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — the model field partTwo.laboratoryTestsRequired carries @NotNull for the validation groups NotificationHighRiskNonChedppFieldValidation and NotificationHighRiskEuCedFieldValidation (ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notificationschema/representation/PartTwo.java:79-87). CHED-D (notification type CED) is validated with NotificationHighRiskNonChedppFieldValidation among its part-two groups (ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notification/validators/field/PartTwoFieldValidator.java:98-104, case CED), so this field IS server-side mandatory for CHED-D in legacy IPAFFS. NOTE (rendered vs model): the frontend renders NO HTML 'required' attribute (confirmed) and pre-selects 'No', so a value is always present at submit and the @NotNull rarely fires in the UI; mandatoriness is enforced server-side, not client-side. This is a legacy policy the rebuild may revisit — whether the record-lab-tests question must be answered at all is a decision-app choice, not an intrinsic data rule.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 123 (Yes, value=true); No default-checked (value=false); corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 128. name=labTestsRequired; ids radio-lab-tests-required-yes / radio-lab-tests-required-no. HTML required attribute absent; No is checked by default. Corroborated by page object page-objects/decision/LabTestsIntroPage.ts:6-10 (radioYes/radioNo getters, role=radio names 'Yes'/'No') and workflow workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:413 (radioYes.check) and :483,:506 (radioNo.check). LEGACY corroboration: the IPAFFS decision-app template renders exactly these two radios (ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/bip/labTestsRequired.html:25-34) — inputs name='labTestsRequired' value='true'/'false', ids radio-lab-tests-required-yes / radio-lab-tests-required-no, legend 'Would you like to record laboratory tests?' (:23). BEHAVIOUR (inferred): 'Yes' opens the laboratory-test sub-journey (commodity select -> commodity test -> commodity sample -> record test information -> review) per ched-d-workflows.ts:413-449; 'No' skips the sub-journey and continues straight to the decision (ched-d-workflows.ts:483-484, :506-507).

fields.name: labTestsRequired.random
fields.label: Random
fields.evidence: RESOLVED BY LEGACY SOURCE (leave as a flagged non-option, do not build): the SHARED laboratory-tests intro page object exposes a THIRD radio option 'Random' (page-objects/decision/LabTestsIntroPage.ts:14-16, radioRandom getter) that NO CHED-D trace rendered. The authoritative IPAFFS decision-app template for THIS page — ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/bip/labTestsRequired.html:25-34 — renders ONLY the Yes/No radios (name='labTestsRequired'); there is NO 'Random' control on the CHED-D lab-tests-required page. 'Random' is instead a laboratoryTests.testReason VALUE selected on a DOWNSTREAM sub-journey page (the labTestReason page — ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/partials/certificate/laboratory_tests.html:26-33, where for CED the 'Random' reason is even hard-checked: {{#eq @root.notification.type 'CED'}}<span class="checkbox checked">). The shared page object's radioRandom getter is driven only by CHED-A workflows (ched-a-workflows.ts:778 'select Random (CHED-A specific)', :1114). CONCLUSION: the CHED-D lab-tests-required page offers only Yes/No — 'Random' does NOT belong on this page. Retained as a documented non-option, not a field to build.

validationMessages.trigger: Server-side @NotNull on partTwo.laboratoryTestsRequired when the CHED-D (CED) part-two validation runs and the field is absent (PartTwo.java:79-87 message key parttwo.laboratorytestsrequired.not.null; CED validation group wiring PartTwoFieldValidator.java:98-104).
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/resources/ValidationMessages.properties:183 — uk.gov.defra.tracesx.notificationschema.representation.parttwo.laboratorytestsrequired.not.null=Are laboratory tests required. Not observed rendering in any trace for this page (no error-state trace captured); this is the exact server-side copy. Legacy mandatoriness the rebuild may revisit (see field requiredEvidence).

validationMessages.message: At least one laboratory test is required
validationMessages.trigger: Fires DOWNSTREAM, not on this page: when 'Yes' is chosen but no laboratory test is subsequently added (reasonLaboratoryTestsNotAdded.not.empty). Recorded here for traceability of the sub-journey this Yes/No opens; belongs to a lab-tests sub-journey page spec.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/resources/ValidationMessages.properties:185 — parttwo.reasonlaboratorytestsnotadded.not.empty=At least one laboratory test is required. Related sibling: :184 parttwo.laboratorytestspending.not.empty=Lab results pending for this consignment. Neither applies to the Yes/No control on THIS page; they gate the sub-journey ('Yes' branch).

openQuestions: RESOLVED (legacy): the radio group has no HTML 'required' attribute and 'No' is pre-selected, so no client-side validation fires. Server-side, partTwo.laboratoryTestsRequired IS @NotNull for CHED-D (CED validated with NotificationHighRiskNonChedppFieldValidation — PartTwo.java:79-87, PartTwoFieldValidator.java:98-104), with copy 'Are laboratory tests required' (ValidationMessages.properties:183, now in validationMessages). Because 'No' pre-selects a value, this @NotNull rarely fires in the real UI. Open policy question for the rebuild: should answering the record-lab-tests question be mandatory at all, or is a default-No acceptable?

This is a post-submission inspector page reached from the Decision Hub via a 'Laboratory tests' link (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:410, :481, :504 — pages.decisionHub.linkLaboratoryTests.click()), consistent with the trace's Percy Inspector-Tester / 'Record decision' menu context. Selecting 'Yes' opens a laboratory-test sub-journey; the downstream pages were not captured in these two traces, but the QA page objects and ched-d-workflows.ts:416-449 confirm the sub-journey surface (LabTestsCommoditySelectPage, LabTestsCommodityTestPage, LabTestsCommoditySamplePage, RecordLaboratoryTestInformationPage, LabTestsReviewPage) — each is a separate page spec / gap for tracing.

RESOLVED (legacy): the shared page object exposes a third radio option 'Random' (LabTestsIntroPage.ts:14-16) driven only by CHED-A workflows. The authoritative IPAFFS decision-app template for THIS page (ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/bip/labTestsRequired.html:25-34) renders ONLY Yes/No — there is no 'Random' control on the CHED-D lab-tests-required page. 'Random' is a laboratoryTests.testReason value on a downstream sub-journey page, hard-checked for CED (laboratory_tests.html:26-33). The rebuild should build only Yes/No here.

The page object exposes only a 'Save and continue' button (LabTestsIntroPage.ts:18-20), yet the trace rendered TWO submit buttons ('Save and return' and 'Save and continue', both name='save-button'). Rendered reality wins (both buttons confirmed); the QA automation simply never drives 'Save and return' on this page — not a contradiction, but noted so the rebuild retains both actions.

The 'Choose from:' hint is visually hidden and carries a mis-named id (container-seals-hint) copied from another template; confirm the rebuild drops it rather than reproducing the artifact. LEGACY corroboration: the leaked id is genuinely in the IPAFFS source — ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/bip/labTestsRequired.html:24 hard-codes
Choose from:
. The trace and the source agree; this is a real IPAFFS artifact, not a trace glitch.
